content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

SUPERSEDED BY 1998
AMENDMENTS TO
REGULATION 18530

September 5, 1990


Keith S. Fraser
Varni, Fraser, Hartwell & Rodgers
2109 Fourth Street
P.O. Box 511
Livermore, CA 94550-4551

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of
Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-90-530

Dear Mr. Fraser:

We have received your letter dated July 13, 1990 seeking
formal written confirmation of telephone advice provided to you on
August 3, 1990. As counsel for the Livermore-Amador Valley
Transit Authority, you sought advice concerning the Authority's
obligations under the Political Reform Act ("the Act") with
respect to its membership in the local chamber of commerce.
Because your letter does not refer to a specific pending
governmental decision, we are treating your request as one for
informal assistance only. (Regulation 18329.)² Your letter
generally reflects the advice provided to you, but some
clarification is necessary.

On the basis of the facts you provided, you were advised that
the Act did not preclude the Authority from continuing its
membership in the Livermore Chamber of Commerce even though the
Chamber had recently amended its by-laws to permit it to endorse
ballot issues and candidates for elective office.

The Act prohibits a public office from expending, and a
candidate from accepting, public moneys for the purpose of seeking
elective office. (Section 85300.) In the event the Authority's
membership in the Chamber could somehow be construed as
implicating this prohibition as a result of the amended by-laws,

_______________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. All refer-
ences to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California
Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. I-90-530
Page 2


you were advised that the Chamber could keep its collected
membership fees separate from those funds used for the purpose of
supporting or opposing candidates for elective office. Other than
this section, we are not aware of any other provisions of the Act
that are implicated by the Chamber's by-law amendment concerning
the Authority's membership.

The advice you were provided is limited to the provisions of
the Act and does not address other laws that might apply to the
situation described in your query.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel /

[signature]
By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:plh

sections.question_synthetic: May a public transit authority continue its membership in a local chamber of commerce after the chamber amended its bylaws to permit endorsement of ballot issues and candidates, and does this implicate the Political Reform Act's restrictions on public expenditures for elective office?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Act does not preclude the Authority from continuing its chamber membership even with the amended bylaws. The chamber may keep membership fees separate from funds used to support or oppose candidates. No other provisions of the Act are implicated by this arrangement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers in document. Confirmation letter for prior telephone advice. Informal assistance only (Regulation 1-a329) - does not provide immunity like formal written advice. Legal question and conclusion synthesized from body text. Document appears to have OCR artifacts but content is comprehensible.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May a public transit authority continue its membership in a local chamber of commerce after the chamber amended its bylaws to permit endorsement of ballot issues and candidates, and does this implicate the Political Reform Act's restrictions on public expenditures for elective office?

CONCLUSION: The Act does not preclude the Authority from continuing its chamber membership even with the amended bylaws. The chamber may keep membership fees separate from funds used to support or oppose candidates. No other provisions of the Act are implicated by this arrangement.

embedding.summary: FPPC provides informal written confirmation of prior telephone advice to counsel for the Livermore-Amador Valley Transit Authority regarding the Authority's continued membership in the Livermore Chamber of Commerce following the chamber's amendment of bylaws to permit candidate and ballot issue endorsements.
